title: Dynamic Rendering Decision and Isolated Browser-Service Boundary · Execution Trace: Browser Lifecycle Proof
summary: The Go crawler cannot satisfy one dynamic requirement, so it shells out to an uncontrolled browser process for every page and trusts returned HTML. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go browser lifecycle proof increment in least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Require context isolation, closure, timeout, crash, leak, queue, version, and capacity evidence from the service without claiming it from Go models.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Browser Lifecycle Proof model · Theory Browser Lifecycle…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 8301 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove render necessity, typed browser port, subresource policy, untrusted results, and lifecycle evidence; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require context isolation, closure, timeout, crash, leak, queue, version, and capacity evidence from the service without claiming it from Go models.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Browser Lifecycle Proof model · Theory Browser Lifecycle…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Closing the RPC response proves browser contexts and processes closed.
